custom_rules.text: Always use optional chaining when reading currentBlock: currentBlock?.data?.columns, currentBlock?.data?.data. Never assume the data is populated.
Rows in currentBlock.data.data are positional arrays, not objects. Always use cols.indexOf('column_name') to get values — never row.column_name.
Never load amCharts scripts directly in a chart block. Always wrap all am5 code in window.AMCHARTS_LOADER.load().then(function() {...}).
Always dispose of the amCharts root before re-rendering: if (root) root.dispose(); at the top of the render function to prevent ghost charts stacking on filter changes.
When the block's query uses group_by, the data is already aggregated. Do not attempt to re-aggregate it in JavaScript.
Add a const DEBUG = true flag at the top of every block. Wrap all console.log calls in if (DEBUG) {...} so debugging can be toggled off without removing log statements.
Always use CSS variables (--color-primary, --color-text, --body-bg-color, --color-success, --color-danger) instead of hardcoded colors.
Always give chart container divs unique IDs (chartdiv-1, chartdiv-2, etc.)
Always add a visible error message in the block UI if data fails to load — never fail silently.
